agent_outputs.trading_analyst:
MACRO: BULLISH
SPY and QQQ both trading above 200d MAs in a confirmed TRENDING_UP regime. VIX at 19.0 is stable and non-threatening. Broad gap-ups across IWM (+1.19%), XLK (+1.01%), XLV (+1.26%), XLI (+1.13%) signal risk-on breadth. Energy (XLE -1.47%) and utilities (XLU -0.76%) are the only laggards — classic rotation into growth/cyclicals. No high-impact macro events today; Retail Sales Tuesday is the next catalyst.

OPPORTUNITIES:

SYMBOL: IWM
DIRECTION: LONG
CONVICTION: HIGH
RATIONALE: Small caps gapping up 1.19% with the strongest pre-market move among index ETFs, confirming risk-on broadening beyond mega-cap tech. IWM scores 47pt STRONG_BUY with top marks on trend and multi-timeframe alignment. Volume at 1.5x ADV shows genuine participation. Small caps have lagged large caps for months — this is a catch-up rotation trade in a trending-up regime.
INSTRUMENT: call
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: Small caps are more sensitive to rising Treasury yields (10Y crept to 4.32%), and Tuesday's Retail Sales could disappoint, hitting cyclicals hardest.
KEY RISK: Rate-sensitive; any hawkish Fed Waller comments Tuesday could reverse the move.

SYMBOL: GOOGL
DIRECTION: LONG
CONVICTION: MEDIUM
RATIONALE: Multiple bullish AI-investment articles catalyzing sentiment. Scores 22pt but packs strong internals: trend=8, momentum=8, squeeze=10, flow=6, multi-timeframe=10. The squeeze score of 10 suggests a volatility contraction about to resolve — in a TRENDING_UP regime that resolution is overwhelmingly upward. Trading at $340 with 1.3x volume and sitting right at VWAP. Not held currently and benefits from the same tech rotation powering XLK +1%.
INSTRUMENT: call
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: Antitrust/regulatory overhang remains a persistent headwind for Alphabet specifically, and AI spending scrutiny could surface any time.
KEY RISK: Earnings season surprises from peers could shift sector sentiment quickly; also exposed to any broader tariff escalation headlines.

SYMBOL: AAPL
DIRECTION: LONG
CONVICTION: MEDIUM
RATIONALE: 21pt STRONG_BUY with perfect trend (10) and momentum (10) scores, trading at $273.92 with 2.2x average volume — the highest volume multiple among large-cap scored signals. Breakout confirmation with strong multi-timeframe alignment (10). The India antitrust news is a sideshow relative to the technical momentum. Broad tech rotation day favors this name.
INSTRUMENT: stock
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: India antitrust headline is a minor negative, but more importantly AAPL's flow score is only 3 — institutional participation may be weaker than the volume suggests (could be retail-driven).
KEY RISK: Tariff exposure on hardware supply chain; any escalation in US-China trade tensions hits AAPL disproportionately.

SYMBOL: F
DIRECTION: LONG
CONVICTION: MEDIUM
RATIONALE: Ford scores 40pt BUY with a perfect squeeze score (10) and strong momentum (10), suggesting a coiled spring ready to move. Volume 1.6x ADV with price already +2.2% above VWAP shows buyers leaning in aggressively. Cyclical/industrial rotation day (XLI +1.13%) supports auto names. At $12.88 it's a low-dollar, high-liquidity vehicle for the broadening trade.
INSTRUMENT: stock
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: Auto sector faces tariff risk and margin pressure from rising input costs; Ford's own fundamentals have been mediocre, and this could be a short-lived squeeze.
KEY RISK: Tuesday Retail Sales miss would hammer consumer cyclicals; tariff headlines on auto imports are a binary risk.

SYMBOL: SPXS
DIRECTION: SHORT
CONVICTION: MEDIUM
RATIONALE: SPXS is a 3x inverse S&P ETF — shorting it is a leveraged long on the market. It scores 44pt SELL with 3.7x ADV volume, the highest volume conviction signal on the board. In a TRENDING_UP regime with broad gap-ups, this inverse product should continue decaying. Trend, momentum, and multi-timeframe all max or near-max bearish readings.
INSTRUMENT: stock
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: Leveraged inverse ETFs have borrowing costs and can be hard to short; any sudden market reversal (e.g., tariff headline) creates outsized losses on a short-inverse position.
KEY RISK: Flash crash or exogenous shock; the 3x leverage works against you violently in a sell-off.

SYMBOL: CVX
DIRECTION: SHORT
CONVICTION: LOW
RATIONALE: Energy is today's clear sector laggard with XLE -1.47%. CVX scores 35pt SELL with notably high IV rank (10) and insider signal (8) suggesting distribution. Oil at $100.72 may be peaking as demand concerns build. The TRENDING_UP regime in equities doesn't extend to energy today — classic sector rotation out of defensives/commodities into growth.
INSTRUMENT: put
COUNTER-ARGUMENT: WTI crude rose from $98 to $101 last week; any Middle East escalation or supply disruption could spike oil and lift CVX rapidly.
KEY RISK: Geopolitical oil supply shock; also Tuesday's crude inventory data could surprise bullishly.
